putIn.name: Peltier Lake public boat launch (Lino Lakes)

takeOut.name: Long Lake Regional Park / Long Lake (New Brighton)

result.explanation: Rice Creek looks like a skip today. The gauge is inside the preferred window at 7.40 ft. +0.14 ft over the last 24h. Stable flow inside the ideal window is what we want. Wind looks noticeable at about 13 mph. Rain odds are high enough to change the trip call. Rain looks imminent in the next few hours. Air temperature is near freezing at 34°F, which lowers same-day trip quality more in the shoulder season. Confidence is high. The passable 6.30 to 7.90 ft band comes directly from the Rice Creek Watershed District. The remaining uncertainty is mostly wind on the lake crossings and whether a portage is needed at road or fish-barrier crossings.

capReasons: Imminent heavy rain caps today at 65.

factors.label: Difficulty
factors.detail: Technically easy when levels cooperate, but lake exposure, culverts, and occasional portages change the day quality.

factors.label: Seasonality
factors.detail: Late spring through early fall is the practical window. Lake wind matters almost as much as the gauge on this route.

checklist.label: Weather window
checklist.detail: No storm signal is showing in the next 12 hours. Air temperature is around 34°F. Peak wind looks near 13 mph. Rain risk peaks around 92%.

checklist.label: Skill and access
checklist.detail: Confirm put-in, take-out, and any posted launch or parking rules before you leave a vehicle.

outlooks.label: Tomorrow
outlooks.explanation: Gauge is steady enough that today's ideal window call should not shift much on its own. Tomorrow: No thunderstorm signal is showing, 66% rain chance, winds up to 13 mph. Tomorrow is an early read, not a promise.

outlooks.label: Weekend (Apr 4-Apr 5)
outlooks.explanation: Gauge is steady enough that today's ideal window call should not shift much on its own. Weekend (Apr 4-Apr 5): No thunderstorm signal is showing, 86% rain chance, winds up to 17 mph. Weekend outlooks stay a little more conservative.
